raw_action_dict.reasoning: Track curves right in image but waypoint analysis shows delta +36.3° meaning the target waypoint is to the LEFT — car heading is too far south and needs to turn left/northeast to follow the track centerline.
knowledge: Track loops sharply south around waypoints 103-115 then curves back northeast
After waypoint 106, track runs nearly due east at y≈0.68 — car must steer left strongly
Positive delta = steer left, negative delta = steer right
At delta +30-50°, use steering_angle=+15 to +20 at speed 1.0-1.5
Car is left of center (0.21m) — within safe bounds at track width 0.75m
Waypoints 106-120 run east along y≈0.68-0.69 — long straight ahead
After sharp south loop, car must aggressively turn left to follow eastward track
Avoid oversteer oscillation on straights; use smallest valid angle when delta < 10°
Previous -15 steering was wrong direction for this segment — delta was actually positive
Watch for transition from southward heading to eastward heading around waypoint 106
